title: flowstate -- Structure
text: Flow Cytometry Standard files read parsed flowstate (S3 object). flowstate primarily comprised following parsed (named list) elements – data.table::data.table(): Included package – purpose example(s) – (subsampled) .fcs files acquired Cytek Aurora (5L) using SpectroFlo software. Read/parse flowstate using flowstate::read.flowstate().
code: ## paths to example .fcs files fcs.files <- system.file("extdata", package = "flowstate") |>    list.files(full.names = T) |>    grep(pattern = "BLOCK.*.fcs", value = T) fcs.files |> basename() |> print() #> [1] "COVAIL_002_CYTOKINE_BLOCK1_1.fcs" "COVAIL_002_CYTOKINE_BLOCK1_2.fcs" #> [3] "COVAIL_002_CYTOKINE_BLOCK1_3.fcs"  ## read them in and concatenate; a flowstate object fs <- flowstate::read.flowstate(   fcs.file.paths = fcs.files,   colnames.type = "S",   concatenate = TRUE ) #> COVAIL_002_CYTOKINE_BLOCK1_1.fcs --> flowstate #> COVAIL_002_CYTOKINE_BLOCK1_2.fcs --> flowstate #> COVAIL_002_CYTOKINE_BLOCK1_3.fcs --> flowstate #> Concatenating 'flowstates'...  ## S3 object of class "flowstate" class(fs) #> [1] "flowstate"  ## flowstate structure names(fs) #> [1] "data"       "parameters" "keywords"   "spill"

title: flowstate -- Structure
text: Data can accessed name via: fs$data fs[['data']]. [['data']] contains events rows instrument measurements (Time, scatter, fluorescence, etc.) columns. design, transformation applied linear measurement values reading. addition, unique sample identifier (factored) added workflow purposes. [['data']] also contains column/variable-specific attribute metadata – notably, alias attribute (named character vector) serves purpose linking syntactically-valid names original (required unique) ‘$PnN’ name. aliases used rename variables '[['data']], allowing non-standard evaluation programming using data.table.
code: ## fs$data[] ## column/variable names names(fs$data) #>  [1] "Time"      "SSC_W"     "SSC_H"     "SSC_A"     "FSC_W"     "FSC_H"     #>  [7] "FSC_A"     "SSCB_W"    "SSCB_H"    "SSCB_A"    "CD45RA"    "CD45RO"    #> [13] "TCRgd"     "CD45BC1"   "IL2"       "CD8"       "CD197"     "CD45BC2"   #> [19] "CD45BC3"   "CD57"      "CD193"     "CD45BC4"   "CD127"     "CD56"      #> [25] "CD199"     "CD49a"     "GranzymeB" "CD45BC5"   "CD95"      "CD3"       #> [31] "CD4"       "CD69"      "TNFa"      "CD183"     "IFNg"      "CD103"     #> [37] "CD45BC6"   "CD122"     "ia4b7"     "CD45BC7"   "Viability" "CD27"      #> [43] "KLRG1"     "sample.id"  ## sample identifiers fs$data[, levels(sample.id)] #> [1] "COVAIL_002_CYTOKINE_BLOCK1_1" "COVAIL_002_CYTOKINE_BLOCK1_2" #> [3] "COVAIL_002_CYTOKINE_BLOCK1_3" ## example alias attribute fs$data[, attr(CD3, which = 'alias')] #>         N         S   N.alias   S.alias  #> "RB744-A"     "CD3"   "RB744"     "CD3"

title: flowstate -- Structure
text: Parameters can accessed name via: fs$parameters fs[['parameters']]. [['parameters']] contains instrument-specific measurement parameters – keyword-value pairs denoted ‘$P|P’ (N, S, V, TYPE, etc.). [['parameters']] also contains attribute-level metadata – notably, parameters.diff attribute (data.table) serves purpose preserving sample-specific differences due unique measurement values (e.g., differences measured ‘Time’).
code: ## fs$parameters ## pd <- attr(fs$parameters, 'parameters.diff') rmarkdown::paged_table(pd)

title: flowstate -- Structure
text: Keywords can accessed name via: fs$keywords fs[['keywords']]. [['keywords']] contains instrument/sample-specific metadata. information contained leveraged number different actions, : platform/cytometer identification; extracting adding unique identifiers [['data']]; adding/constructing new keywords purpose analysis/workflow; QC/diagnostics. Following best practices laid Flow Cytometry Standard, series keywords added mark flowstate object(s) modified – longer source data. inclusion keywords ('$LAST_MODIFIED', '$LAST_MODIFIER', '$ORIGINALITY') ensures transparency indication explicitly modified.
code: ## fs$keywords ## keywords to indicate/track modification fs$keywords[, .(`$LAST_MODIFIED`,`$LAST_MODIFIER`,`$ORIGINALITY`)] #>             $LAST_MODIFIED   $LAST_MODIFIER $ORIGINALITY #>                     <char>           <char>       <char> #> 1: 21-JUL-2026 20:56:44.52 flowstate_0.17.1 DataModified #> 2: 21-JUL-2026 20:56:44.60 flowstate_0.17.1 DataModified #> 3: 21-JUL-2026 20:56:44.66 flowstate_0.17.1 DataModified

title: flowstate -- Structure
text: Spill can accessed name via: fs$spill fs[['spill']]. [['spill']] contains instrument/sample-specific spill() matrix – encoded. can modified correction values applied [['data']] (try ) fix unmixing/compensation errors. Unless explicitly modified user, default matrix contain correction values. N.B.: software manufacturers – least SpectroFlo – write small value (1E-6) first matrix pair; think overcome bug FlowJo handles matrix…
code: ## fs$spill ## small value; there in the source data fs$spill[2,1] #>    CD45RA #>     <num> #> 1:  1e-06

what: Flow Cytometry Standard (FCS) Processing, Analysis, and Visualization
title: Flow Cytometry Standard (FCS) Processing, Analysis, and Visualization
text: flowstate provides system processing, analyzing, visualizing Flow Cytometry Standard (FCS) data. primary features flowstate: Read parse .fcs files flowstate objects (S3) Perform spectral unmixing (Full Spectrum Cytometry/Spectral Cytometry) Perform spillover compensation/correction Transform expression values (MFI/ion counts) Visualize primary data Write FCS version 3.1 compliant files

title: Flow Cytometry Standard (FCS) Processing, Analysis, and Visualization
text: can install flowstate GitHub :
code: pak::pak("nlaniewski/flowstate") ## or the development version: pak::pak("nlaniewski/flowstate@dev")

title: Flow Cytometry Standard (FCS) Processing, Analysis, and Visualization
text: Paths previously acquired .fcs files: Read/concatenate: Transform: Visualize:  Compensate:
code: fcs.file.paths <- system.file("extdata", package = "flowstate") |>   list.files(full.names = TRUE, pattern = "BLOCK.*.fcs") fs <- flowstate::read.flowstate(   fcs.file.paths,   colnames.type = "S",   concatenate = TRUE ) #> COVAIL_002_CYTOKINE_BLOCK1_1.fcs --> flowstate #> COVAIL_002_CYTOKINE_BLOCK1_2.fcs --> flowstate #> COVAIL_002_CYTOKINE_BLOCK1_3.fcs --> flowstate #> Concatenating 'flowstates'... flowstate::flowstate.transform(fs) #> flowstate --> transforming... plot(fs, CD3, Viability) +    ggplot2::guides(fill = 'none') +    ggplot2::facet_wrap(~sample.id) ##needs compensation correction plot(fs, CD4, CD8) + ggplot2::guides(fill = 'none') ##update [['spill']] with a correction value flowstate::spillover.update.value(fs, CD8, CD4, 0.03) ##apply spillover correction flowstate::spillover.apply(fs) #> flowstate --> transforming -- inverse... #> flowstate --> transforming... ##compensated data plot(fs, CD4, CD8) + ggplot2::guides(fill = 'none')

The package "hexbin" is required for `stat_bin_hex()`.   #apply compensation spillover.apply(fs) #> flowstate --> transforming -- inverse... #> flowstate --> transforming...  #compensated data plot(fs, CD4, CD8) + ggplot2::labs(title = "Compensated") #> Warning: Computation failed in `stat_binhex()`. #> Caused by error in `compute_group()`: #> ! The package "hexbin" is required for `stat_bin_hex()`.   #return data values to original state by decompensating spillover.apply(fs, decompensate = TRUE) #> flowstate --> transforming -- inverse... #> flowstate --> transforming...  plot(fs, CD4, CD8) + ggplot2::labs(title = "Over-compensated") #> Warning: Computation failed in `stat_binhex()`. #> Caused by error in `compute_group()`: #> ! The package "hexbin" is required for `stat_bin_hex()`.

what: Write a flowstate as a Flow Cytometry Standard file (FCS 3.1) — write.flowstate
title: Write a flowstate as a Flow Cytometry Standard file (FCS 3.1) — write.flowstate
text: flowstate – following Flow Cytometry Standard conventions – converted FCS 3.1 written disk. conversion follows: [['keywords']], [['parameters']], [['spill']] (present) converted character string using delimiter "|" separate keyword-value pairs. string forms TEXT segment FCS file. [['data']] written row-major binary. forms DATA segment FCS file. HEADER segment (string) – containing required offsets – derived TEXT DATA. FCS 3.1 compliant file written disk. N.B.: testing, FCS 3.1 files written disk write.flowstate fully compatible read.flowstate flowCore::read.FCS; issues proprietary software (.e., FlowJo). proprietary software used FCS 3.1 files generated write.flowstate, caution advised.

title: Write a flowstate as a Flow Cytometry Standard file (FCS 3.1) — write.flowstate

text: flowstate flowstate returned read.flowstate. file.dir file.path – default tempdir; file directory saving output FCS 3.1 file(s). fil.suffix Character string – default NULL; defined, suffix appended endianness Character string – default "little"; see endian.

title: Write a flowstate as a Flow Cytometry Standard file (FCS 3.1) — write.flowstate
text: FCS 3.1 file(s) written disk summary message displayed. original $FIL appended 'flowstateMOD' explicit indicator modification; fil.suffix defined, 'flowstateMOD' appended string value. UPDATES REFERENCE: flowstate[['data']] – non-numeric (logical /factor) columns converted numeric flowstate[['keywords']] – non-character (factor) columns converted character flowstate[['spill']] – column names matched/updated using [['parameters']][['N']] UPDATES ASSIGNMENT environment: flowstate[['parameters']] – [['parameters']] updated row-binding derived columns found [['data']] Invisibly returns flowstate.
